bead.title: fix(agent): TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle flakes under load
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle in
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195 passes
  3/3 in isolation but fails reliably during drain-induced load (e.g.,
  observed twice during the 2026-05-09 drain at 19:22:43 and 19:30:52
  while the drain was processing ddx-94a368b6 with concurrent test
  workloads on the same machine).

  Failure surfaces as `--- FAIL: TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNext…`
  during lefthook pre-push full-tests; pushes get rejected and the
  drain self-recovers via push-race retry, wasting one round of
  full-tests (~168s).

  Note: after sister bead ddx-f1c476c3 drops full-tests from pre-push,
  this flake stops blocking drain pushes (it would only fire in CI).
  But the underlying flake is still real and should be fixed for CI
  reliability.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195-262.
  The test wires:
    - A worker in watch mode with IdleInterval=time.Hour.
    - A buffered progressCh listener goroutine that, on loop.idle,
      creates a new ready bead and sends to wakeCh.
    - The worker is expected to pick up the new bead and the executor
      callback cancels context; the test then asserts the stdout log
      contains a "taking next ready bead" transition line.

  The flake mode is goroutine scheduling under contention:
    - Under heavy load (e.g., a parallel drain + lefthook full-tests),
      the goroutine listening on progressCh can be delayed beyond the
      worker's loop iteration window.
    - The worker may exit watch mode (via its context cancel from a
      different code path) before the goroutine has a chance to send
      the wake signal, so the "next ready bead" transition log line
      is never emitted.
    - Or: the wake signal is sent but the worker's select-loop picks a
      different branch first under load.

  The test also depends on a fixed-time injector (`fixedWatchStdoutTime`)
  for its log-line assertion, which assumes precise ordering between
  the test's bead creation and the worker's output. Under load, that
  ordering can flip without invalidating the actual behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
  Investigate cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195-262
  with the goal of removing load-sensitivity:

  1. Replace the goroutine-listener pattern with a deterministic
     synchronization barrier — e.g., the test could explicitly wait
     for the worker to enter loop.idle (via a synchronous channel
     receive from progressCh) before creating the new bead, rather
     than handling the event in a separate goroutine.

  2. If the test must stay async, increase the buffer on progressCh
     and wakeCh so a slow goroutine doesn't drop signals; assert
     post-hoc on the buffered channel contents rather than racing the
     worker's exit.

  3. Use t.Parallel() if appropriate, but only after confirming the
     test's t.Setenv / store fixtures are parallel-safe.

  4. As a minimum: add a t.Helper()-decorated retry wrapper for the
     log-line assertion so a load-induced miss can re-poll briefly,
     OR mark the test as testing.Short()-skip if it cannot be made
     deterministic (NOT preferred — fix it properly).

NON-SCOPE
  - Refactoring the watch-mode logging system itself (the production
    behavior is correct; only the test's assertion harness is flaky).
  - Other flaky tests elsewhere in the suite.
  - Adding a load-simulation test (separate concern).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-f1c476c3 (drop full-tests from pre-push) reduces
    the urgency of this fix because the flake no longer blocks drain
    pushes — but it still blocks CI on every push to origin.
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195 TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle is restructured to remove load-sensitivity (deterministic synchronization barrier OR documented retry wrapper).
2. cd cli && go test -count=10 -run TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle ./internal/agent/... green (10 consecutive passes proves determinism).
3. cd cli && go test -count=3 -race -run TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle ./internal/agent/... green under simulated load (e.g., a separate `go test ./...` running concurrently in another shell).
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
